alignments.candidate_evidence: Candidate positioned as Strategy and Operations / Product Operations leader with eight years at Google Devices and Services with two promotions, including scaling global commercial operations and transformation programs.
alignments.job_requirement: Strategy & Operations department role with CEO Office context

gaps.gap: JD is corrupted, incomplete, and unintelligible. No actual job responsibilities, requirements, scope, reporting structure, or business context are discernible.
gaps.mitigation: Request uncorrupted, full JD text. Cannot assess fit without knowing what the role actually entails.

gaps.gap: Title 'Strategy, CEO Office' suggests potential over-leveling (CEO Office / Chief of Staff equivalent). Candidate's seniority ceiling rules typically target L4–L5 (Manager, Senior Manager, Lead), not C-suite support unless startup or very early-stage.
gaps.mitigation: Clarify company stage and whether this is a strategic cross-functional coordinator role (L4) or a senior advisory/CoS role requiring VP+ context.

gaps.gap: No clarity on whether role is core strategy & operations execution (candidate-aligned) versus political/advisory C-suite support or investor/board relations.
gaps.mitigation: JD must specify scope: business unit strategy? transformation program leadership? functional operations? or senior staff/advisory?

output.summary: JD is corrupted and unreadable—only title "Strategy, CEO Office" and company context visible. Cannot evaluate role fit, scope, or requirements without a clean, full job description. Title suggests potential over-leveling (CEO Office roles often L5+ or equivalent). Recommend obtaining uncorrupted JD and clarifying reporting structure, team scope, and core responsibilities before proceeding.
uncertainties: JD snapshot is HTML/CSS/JS garbage with no readable job description content
Checkout.com stage, size, and organization structure not provided; cannot assess CEO Office role seniority anchor
No information on whether role reports to CEO, CFO, COO, or other executive; critical for level calibration
Cannot distinguish between strategic execution, functional operations, program leadership, and advisory/staff duties
